name: Money Manager External API
description: External REST-style API for Money Tracker. Support: freemanzk#gmail.com
readme: All endpoints use HTTP POST with application/x-www-form-urlencoded body. Copy your API token from the mobile app (Settings → API, premium required) and pass it as the form field token on each request. Use personal endpoints for the user's own ledger; use cashbook endpoints for shared team books. Transaction responses mix millisecond and second Unix timestamps — see timestamp_fields. Interactive tester: https://money.quhou123.com/Api/test

request_format.note: Do not send a JSON request body. Pass parameters as form fields.

response_envelope.description: Every endpoint returns a JSON object with this shape.

schema.msg: string — human-readable message, e.g. success or error reason
schema.data: mixed — payload on success; may be absent on failure
schema.meta: object — optional; present on getTransactions / getCashbookTransactions query mode

authentication.summary: Every API request (except this documentation page) must include your API token.

how_to_pass.note: Pass token in the same form body as other parameters; do not use a separate Authorization header.

where_to_get_token.usage: Copy the token shown on that page into your integration or the interactive tester at https://money.quhou123.com/Api/test

authentication.validity: Long-lived: remains valid until you tap regenerate in the app (Settings → API).
authentication.revocation: Regenerating in the app invalidates the previous token immediately.
authentication.cannot_use: The app login session (Auth-Token header used inside the mobile app) is not accepted by these external endpoints.

default_mode.trigger: Omit start_date, end_date, offset (or offset=0), type, category_id, and account_id
default_mode.limit: Default 50; passing limit alone only changes count (max 500), does not switch modes

query_mode.trigger: Any of: start_date, end_date, offset>0, type, category_id, account_id
query_mode.meta: Returned (total, limit, offset, has_more, optional start_date/end_date)
query_mode.note: Passing only type (no dates) filters all matching records in history, not just the latest 50

date_range_rules.format: YYYY-MM-DD for start_date / end_date; month and day may be non-zero-padded (e.g. 2026-1-5)
date_range_rules.same_as_add_date: Same parsing rules as addTransaction / addCashbookTransaction date parameter
date_range_rules.timezone: Inclusive day range uses server default timezone when mapping to date_time (ms); see timestamp_fields.date_filter_timezone
date_range_rules.meta_echo: meta.start_date / meta.end_date are normalized to zero-padded YYYY-MM-DD
date_range_rules.max_span_applies_when: Both start_date and end_date are provided
date_range_rules.read_note: get* may return type values beyond 1/2 (e.g. transfers) even though add* only writes income/expense

rate_limits_and_quotas.rate_limit_exceeded_msg: Too many requests, please try later
rate_limits_and_quotas.transaction_date_range_max_days_note: Enforced only when both start_date and end_date are sent

pagination_pattern.description: For date-range transaction export, loop until meta.has_more is false.
pagination_pattern.pseudo_code: offset=0; repeat { POST getTransactions with limit=500, offset; offset += limit } while (meta.has_more)

unsupported_capabilities.update_transaction: No API to edit an existing transaction
unsupported_capabilities.delete_transaction: No API to delete a transaction
unsupported_capabilities.transfer_records: addTransaction / addCashbookTransaction only accept type 1 (income) or 2 (expense), not transfers
unsupported_capabilities.statistics_or_reports: No server-side summary endpoint — aggregate in your client after getTransactions
unsupported_capabilities.incremental_sync_cursor: No server_update_time cursor API for external clients (field is Unix seconds); use date-range query or default latest-50

timestamp_fields.summary: Transaction objects use mixed Unix timestamp units. Always check the field name before parsing.

milliseconds.date_time: Ledger entry date/time (also drives query-mode date filters after server converts start_date/end_date)
milliseconds.add_time: Client-side creation time when the record was first saved on a device
milliseconds.update_time: Client-side last modification time on a device

date_filter_timezone.rule: start_date / end_date calendar boundaries use PHP strtotime on the server default timezone (start day 00:00:00, end day 23:59:59), then compare to date_time (milliseconds)
date_filter_timezone.not_utc_calendar: Calendar-day filtering is not fixed to UTC unless the server itself runs in UTC
date_filter_timezone.epoch_fields: add_time / update_time / server_* are Unix epochs; use field-specific units when converting (see parse_examples)

seconds.server_add_time: Server-side first persist time (Unix epoch seconds)
seconds.server_update_time: Server-side last update time; default latest-50 list sorts by this field (seconds)

write_requests.addTransaction_date_param: Pass calendar field date as YYYY-MM-DD (month/day may be non-zero-padded, e.g. 2026-1-5). Do not send date_time, add_time, update_time, or server_* on write
write_requests.server_sets_timestamps: add* responses include timestamp fields set by the server

timestamp_fields.digit_heuristic: Rough rule: 13-digit integers are milliseconds; 10-digit integers are seconds

parse_examples.note: Examples below convert epoch fields only. They do not change calendar start_date/end_date filter boundaries (see date_filter_timezone).

decision_tree.question: Are you reading or writing a shared team / family cashbook?
decision_tree.yes: Use cashbook_* endpoints. cashbook_id is required. Call getCashbookList to discover IDs.
decision_tree.no: Use personal endpoints (getTransactions, addTransaction, getCategories, getAccounts). Do not pass cashbook_id.

decision_tree.question: Do you need default currency hints when posting amounts?
decision_tree.yes: Call getCurrencyHintDefaults (optional cashbook_id for team books) and/or getCurrencyList.
decision_tree.no: Omit currency_code; amount uses user or cashbook default per add* rules.

currency_and_amount_semantics.summary: On addTransaction / addCashbookTransaction, the request amount is always in an "entry currency". The server stores amount in the book default currency (personal user default or cashbook default) and may also write account_currency_* / foreign_currency_* fields.

write_parameter.format: ISO 4217 three-letter code, e.g. CNY, USD, EUR (must exist in getCurrencyList)
write_parameter.not_uuid: Pass ISO code only — not the currency table UUID. Response fields such as currency_id, account_currency_id, and foreign_currency_id are read-only stored IDs.
write_parameter.optional: Omit when the amount is already in the linked account currency or book default currency

book_default_currency.personal_addTransaction: User default currency — read user_default_currency_code from getCurrencyHintDefaults
book_default_currency.cashbook_addCashbookTransaction: Cashbook default currency — read cashbook_default_currency_code from getCurrencyHintDefaults with cashbook_id
book_default_currency.required_for_conversion: If book default currency is not configured: omitting account_id and currency_code still succeeds and stores amount as-is. If conversion is required (foreign currency or account currency ≠ book default) but book default is missing, request fails.

entry_currency_decision_tree.question: Is currency_code provided?
entry_currency_decision_tree.yes: Entry currency = that ISO code (must exist in getCurrencyList). Request amount is in this currency.

entry_currency_decision_tree.question: Is account_id provided?
entry_currency_decision_tree.yes: Entry currency = that account's currency (see currency_code on getAccounts / getCashbookAccounts). Request amount is in account currency.
entry_currency_decision_tree.no: Entry currency = book default currency from getCurrencyHintDefaults. Request amount is in book default currency.

combination_table.amount_input_means: Book default currency when configured; otherwise raw amount with no conversion
combination_table.stored_amount_field: Same as input when entry equals book default or when no book default is configured
combination_table.typical_extra_fields: If entry currency ≠ book default: foreign_currency_id, foreign_currency_amount

combination_table.stored_amount_field: Converted to book default via currency_exchange (base=book default, target=entry code)

combination_table.typical_extra_fields: account_currency_id, account_currency_amount; foreign_* when entry ≠ account currency

combination_table.amount_input_means: Explicit ISO currency (entry currency; may differ from account currency)
combination_table.typical_extra_fields: account_currency_* and foreign_currency_* per server rules

exchange_rates.source: currency_exchange table: conversion_rate where base_code = book default ISO, target_code = entry ISO

reading_transactions_for_reports.amount: Usually the book-default-currency value — use for cross-category totals in one currency
reading_transactions_for_reports.foreign_currency_amount: Original entry amount when user posted in a non-default foreign currency
reading_transactions_for_reports.account_currency_amount: Amount in linked account currency when an account was specified on write
reading_transactions_for_reports.tip: For expense reports in book default currency, sum amount. Show foreign_currency_amount only when you need the original posted value.

category_data.summary: Category list endpoints return id, title, and type. The title field is NOT always a human display name.

title_field.system_categories: title is often an i18n resource key, e.g. category_food, category_shopping — not localized text
title_field.user_defined_categories: title is usually the custom name the user typed in the app

guidance_for_integrators: Use id as the stable identifier in reports and when calling add* / get* filters (category_id parameter maps to income_expenditure_category_id in stored records).
Do not assume title is English or final UI text.
Optional: maintain your own map for known system keys (category_food → Food) or resolve via your app's locale files if you ship the same key scheme.
When grouping expenses in reports, group by category id; use title as a label with fallback to id if title looks like a key (e.g. starts with category_).

example_row.title: category_food
example_row.display_hint: Treat as key "category_food", not the string "Food", unless you localize it

field_aliases.description: Write request parameters vs persisted / read response field names on transaction objects.

table.notes: add* success responses expose alias category_id only (income_expenditure_category_id is removed)
add* success responses expose alias account_id only (from_account_id is removed). to_account_id may exist on read for transfers but add* APIs do not create transfers

changes: timestamp_fields.date_filter_timezone: expose live server timezone identifier and utc_offset_now
Docs: remove duplicate transaction_timestamp_fields from endpoint examples; document server timezone for date filters
start_date / end_date accept non-zero-padded month/day (e.g. 2026-1-5), same as add* date
Unify calendar parsing in parseApiDateYmd; meta echoes canonical YYYY-MM-DD
Add timestamp_fields section: date_time/add_time/update_time are ms; server_add_time/server_update_time are seconds
Annotate query_modes sort keys with units; add parse examples for integrators
Clarify currency_code is ISO 4217 (e.g. CNY, USD), not UUID; add add* request examples with currency_code
Authentication docs: describe token only (no JWT); clarify source (Settings → API) and form field token
Document long-lived token model; revoke via app regenerate
Add query_modes section; clarify limit-only behavior and date span rules
Fix currency edge cases, field alias notes, and add* response examples
getTransactions / getCashbookTransactions: optional start_date, end_date, limit, offset, filters; query mode returns meta
Default latest-50 mode sorts by server_update_time desc

endpoints.name: Add Transaction
endpoints.description: Add one income or expense to the user's personal ledger.

amount.description: Must be > 0; currency see currency_code / account_id rules

currency_code.description: Optional ISO 4217 code (e.g. CNY, USD, EUR) from getCurrencyList — not currency table UUID

endpoints.name: Get Transactions
endpoints.description: List personal transactions. Default: latest 50 by server_update_time desc (seconds). Query mode when any of start_date, end_date, offset>0, type, category_id, account_id is set — filters/sorts by date_time (milliseconds), returns meta. See top-level timestamp_fields.
endpoints.default_behavior: If start_date, end_date, offset, type, category_id and account_id are all omitted, returns the latest 50 records ordered by server_update_time (Unix seconds). limit alone only changes count (default 50, max 500).

endpoints.name: Get Categories
endpoints.description: Personal income/expense categories (includes system defaults). title may be an i18n key like category_food.

endpoints.name: Get Accounts
endpoints.description: Personal accounts; each row includes currency_id and currency_code (ISO).

endpoints.name: Add Cashbook Transaction
endpoints.description: Add one income or expense to a shared cashbook the user belongs to.

currency_code.description: Optional ISO 4217 code (e.g. CNY, USD, EUR) from getCurrencyList — not currency table UUID

example.curl_with_currency_code: curl -X POST 'https://money.quhou123.com/Api/addCashbookTransaction' -d 'token=YOUR_API_TOKEN' -d 'cashbook_id=1001' -d 'type=2' -d 'amount=49.00' -d 'category_id=372f1424-2945-48d4-bf2c-15ede07a725b' -d 'date=2026-01-15' -d 'currency_code=USD' -d 'remark=Team expense posted in USD'

response_error.msg: User is not a cashbook member

endpoints.name: Get Cashbook Transactions
endpoints.description: Same query semantics as getTransactions but scoped to cashbook_id. Default sort: server_update_time (seconds). Query mode: date_time (milliseconds). See timestamp_fields.
endpoints.default_behavior: If start_date, end_date, offset, type, category_id and account_id are all omitted, returns the latest 50 records for that cashbook ordered by server_update_time (Unix seconds). limit alone only changes count.

endpoints.name: Get Cashbook Accounts
endpoints.description: Accounts for a cashbook (for optional account_id on addCashbookTransaction).

endpoints.name: Get Cashbook List
endpoints.description: Cashbooks the authenticated user has joined.

endpoints.name: Get Currency Hint Defaults
endpoints.description: User default currency; optional cashbook_id adds that book's default.
